description_long: This object is a large solar energy infrastructure installation composed of multiple rectangular photovoltaic panels arranged in a grid formation. The panels are mounted on a robust metallic frame, slightly elevated above a flat base, and tilted at an angle to optimize sunlight capture. The surface of each panel is covered with a grid of blue photovoltaic cells, bordered by metallic frames, and one panel features a visible illustrated label or signage. The supporting structure appears to be made of metal, likely aluminum or steel, providing stability and durability. The installation is designed for stationary use, likely on rooftops or open urban spaces, and includes a small protruding component that may serve as an access or maintenance point. The overall design is modular, allowing for efficient energy generation in urban environments.
description: A large, grid-like array of blue solar panels mounted on a metallic frame, tilted for optimal sunlight capture and featuring a labeled panel.
description_view_0: Side view showing two rows of tilted blue solar panels with visible metallic support frames and a slight elevation above the base.
description_view_1: Frontal view displaying the full array of solar panels in a 3x4 grid, with the metallic structure and base clearly visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the tilt of the panels and the illustrated label on one end panel.
description_view_3: Top-down view revealing the complete grid arrangement of the panels and the illustrated label on the corner panel.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25-30 degrees, orientation appears fixed, azimuth direction not clearly specified
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with metallic (likely aluminum or steel) frame
attribute_array_configuration: 3x4 grid of solar panels arranged in three rows and four columns, closely spaced
